videos.description: This Schiller Institute conference is dedicated to the memory of Lyndon LaRouche. His ideas, his economic method, and his optimistic outlook about the potentials of human creativity for the lasting existence of humanity in the universe, are more relevant than ever. Today, the New Silk Road, joined by more than 130 nations, is the first opportunity for developing countries to overcome poverty and underdevelopment and is the achievement of his vision of a new, more just, world economic order. The core of his program for a new Bretton Woods system, in which the world’s strongest nations must work together to replace the oligarchic system, was, from the beginning, the industrialization of Africa, Asia and Latin America — the realization of the solidarity-building World Land-Bridge.

videos.title: Keynote: We can shape a new era of mankind!

Helga Zepp-LaRouche, Chairwoman of the Schiller Institute addresses the November 16-17, 2019 International Schiller Institute conference in Bad Soden, Germany. 

See full conference proceedings here, https://schillerinstitute.com/the-future-of-humanity-as-a-creative-species/

videos.title: Educating a New Generation with LaRouche's Discoveries
videos.description:
The potential of the individual to add to the power in the universe through creative discovery defined the example of Lyndon LaRouche's life and was the subject of a powerful presentation by Megan Beets and Jason Ross at the Schiller Institute conference November 16, 2019. They  opened with a clip of LaRouche asking, “Where does creativity lie? It does not lie in what most people think. It lies in the ability of the living individual, to create states of existence, that is the design of states of existence, to FORECAST what is going to happen. And that’s the most precious thing, for me, to grasp that! That’s everything for me! And that’s what we should hope for.”

They reviewed LaRouche’s work with the Basement team, a scientific research group initiated by LaRouche in 2006, and concluded with a discussion of how we must use the method of LaRouche to develop a new generation of creative geniuses, if mankind is to survive.
